reviews.review_text:
Smite is like the burger shack in your parents town that you keep coming back to. 

It's a quality game. Lots of fun to be had with 1-5 players. I started with DOTA - moved to HoN - and am here playing SMITE now. 

You'll get bored of it, try and find some new games - but in the end, you'll be back.

+ Lots of gods for lots of play styles
+ Good balance on graphics between fun/fantasy and realism
+ Decently balanced gameplay
+ Lots of nice features

- Needs more game modes (a decent amount right now)
- Takes devs a while to nerf OP gods
- No real balancer in place in case somewhat disconnects

Final thought:

Lots of fun, just try it.

So heres review for the game smite.
Its pretty good, it has ups and downs so imma list em because this is a review or somthing.
Ups;
Is free to play and the DLC content are mostly cosmetic.
It's a well supported game with patches every few weeks constantly tweaking and improving the game.
It's colourful and vibrant.
They include some nice lore from the various mythologies.
The game rewards out of the box thinking, skill shots and teamwork.

Downs;
It's free to play so there is no barrier to entry making trolls a common issue.

Hi rez kinda suck at balancing games, I get its difficult but their style is to make something and then nerf it into the ground. The recently released roman goddess Terra is a good example, Terra is only useful in competitive team games because playing her properly requires a lot of team coordination, if you don't have that team then terra is a waste of a player. They nerfed her 4 times since she was released and didnt touch what made her worth getting in competitive, only made everything else worse, all they needed to do was reduce the amount of cooldown and mana refund her ult offered the team, that was it and she was balanced but they took literally everything else and drove it into the ground. Now she cant do any damage, her crowd control isnt that good (yeah there is a root and a stun but they are too short for anything other than pro teams to make use of), her heal isnt potent enough and lasts too long as well as scaling poorly with power, the monolith that causes the heal can be destroyed far too easily.

Matchmaking, its not the worst system in the world but it really punishes playing alone as you can get put in situations where its 5 solo guys Vs a clan which is more or less a gauranteed loss if the clan are even remotely capable of playing as a team. The game balances based on your win-loss ratio or something with special consideration for kills, this system favours assasins and kill stealing more than playing the game well, it can also force you into teams where the other players are way better than you and you become the weak link in a chain and teams where you are the only good player and cannot drag your team to the win and theres nothing worse than being stuck in one of those situations where you are way out of your league because despite the average skill level of each team being the same one guy on one team is responsible for most of the skill (or lack thereof).

The community, its a free to play game where you can chat via keyboard. This always results in an insufferable community that will do ridiculous things like send death threats for not following the pro leagues meta in a casual game, it doesnt matter if the meta is right or not or even how well you perform, you will still get called a peice of ♥♥♥♥ for it, theres no real consequence for quitting out mid game and because of this people will quit for being beat ONCE, ONCE FFS these people cannot handle being beat despite it being a part of the game. People will also ♥♥♥♥ on you for choosing items that they arent familiar with, actively discrouraging creative thinking and counter building. You can even get ♥♥♥♥ on for being focussed, a good strategy for handling high skill players is focussing them and forcing them to be useless early on and if that happens to you your team are MORE LIKELY to call you ♥♥♥♥ than to take a second to realise the other team are throwing everything they have at you and not eveyone else. They won't adapt to the situation or take advantage of the fact the other team blew their load on you or anything useful they will just ♥♥♥♥♥ and often times quit, and then theres instalocking, you get people who actively refuse to play with the team in a team game, 1 isnt too bad but you get 2-4 instalocking players and its a recipe for disaster, they will also blame you for their failures despite them being the ones doing nothing for the team and they throw games because of these ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hissy fits. The community also has trouble adapting to changing situations and many players are quite predictable, if you can adapt to how others play and spot the patterns its easy to do ♥♥♥♥ like approximate where players with invisibility abilities will go while cloaked (I am very good at that and its always hilarious owning some guy who thinks no one knows where they are), its easy to tell who will become the assasins number one target and its easy to tell when the other team will make a move to try and surprise you.

It's inpenetrable to new players, between the poor balancing, constant tweaking, high skill ceiling and awful community its pretty hard to get into the game and enjoy yourself.

In summary I more or less just went off at how ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ awful the issues this game has can get. It's worth playing if you can get some mates together and form a clan but if not skip it entirely.

Isn't big dl, after you done downloadin through steam, there is no more patch (7.2gb). It has ancient defense (even tho the games mode isnt defense the ancient only) and third-person action element on it. Much recommended to try.

Cons is: you need to unlock a heroes (but dont worry there is also daily mission to do and rotation heroes per week just like LoL) and seems to be not popular around SEA (Or it could be means it filled in certain of times only) , but well u can still play at AUS or EU and i think i didnt had lags on these servers.


This is also for you that can't really play Dota 2, coz mostly of their update isnt for major updates which it depletes your internet quota.
i never liked up down view mobas where you click to shoot and where youre going but i loved the idea of mobas and have since fallen in love with smite. reccomend to anyone considering it

I have only been playing the game for 1 month and have just received a 1 week suspension for I have no clue why. I generally type instead of using the vv prompts, which my buddy said gets you reported sometimes, but other than that, I have no idea what I would have done to get a suspension. 

There is no appeal process nor is there an explanation of what I did wrong so I can avoid doing it again in the future.

Try huffing a bucket or two of paint, maybe drive some nails into a wooden board with your forehead, then finish it up with a shot or two of straight ethanol then maybe you will get within a solid 20-30 iq points of the average player in this game. You will run into some 15 loss streaks while commenting on the newest released champion whose abilities dont even work. Could not possible recommend against this game any more if my life depended on it. You will hate yourself and every single person you ever run into on this game. Every session will leave you with pent up rage you cannot begin to unravel, Every new season they break the entire game in a new attempt to fix the previous break they made. The game revolves around like 10 champions. All who require hitting maybe 2 buttons then you win. Anything requiring even the most basic of skill or thought is nerfed and made useless. If a toddler cannot get a penta, then its wrong by the devs standards. If you enjoy playing the same person without fail, game after game. All while hitting maybe three buttons total. Then have at it. No brain needed. No higher thought process. See red name? Me hit 4. Me get kill. Me still lose game cause other team had more people to hit 4. But me hit 4 good. Am best player.

While the concept is good, the servers lag a lot, community is toxic, and in the next patch, they're installing Easy Anti Cheat, which is spyware on your computer while you're playing the game. And you could get permanently banned for no valid reason because of this, and even if you submit a ticket, they won't take back the ban. DO NOT PLAY THIS GAME.

You Want To Learn MOBA ??
play smite.
thats it..
in all the MOBA's in general.. they have a learning curve which is a bit unrewarding and long to master... not the same with smite in my opinion.. you use "W A S D" to move the characters instead of that mouse cursor, which in my opinion is more comfortable... and its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ 3rd-person.. itstead of top-down MOBA... it provides much more immersion. trust me a must play ... #LOVESMITE
regular skin changes and the ability to ply virtually any god is v cool. very hard to get into skill/ game mechanics wise but once you understand it's a great game.

It's a great game but the devs just do not care about leavers. The game has almost no moderation when it comes to people who leave mid match or rage early on. The playerbase is terrible. Would not recommend if you want to spend your time having fun.

pretty dank moba compared to LoL

EDIT: Press F11 to crash smite 10/10

Happiness Loading... https://gyazo.com/86ce5e2775eff11e8103ad4fe0568e3d?token=e345581ff91cfc21e8087ce22487ca53

EDIT: Rube? WTF are they gonna add next? Finn from adventure time?
EDIT: Overall, its fun compared to LoL. Smite was designed to be fun, whereas LoL was designed to b competitive. You actually dont feel like hanging yourself after a match of smite.

Awesome, entertaining, and thrilling! Just like League it takes time to learn different gods/items, but endless hours of fun! My friends and I definitely enjoy playing this together! Learn from a friend when available, and the god pack is a must. Enjoy!

Give back the org T-screen
stop announcing new poorly developed op gods
stop adding 15skins every month
learn maths
fix the game
fire ajax, bring back kelly
ty


Working for HiRez must be easier than creating a Facebook page :^)
Great game, has a few glitches and crashes every now and then but overall a good game even after 1000+ hours of play I still have things to learn.

I have spent a LOT of time playing this game. And it used to be a lot of fun. It has been many months since playing so I came back and WOW, TOXIC players everywhere!! You can not win, or lose without someone cursing and telling you how bad you are and degrading other players. Only download this game if you like being harassed.

This game has it all. Random crits, random stuns, random ally disconnections, random ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, etc. But no matter how much this game gets me frustrated, no matter how much screaming a bad matchup may make me; I cannot in good faith call SMITE a bad game or an unenjoyable one. Much to the hindrance of my sanity & wallet (thanks VShojo collab), I love not only the idea of the game but also the execution. No other game lets me play as Baron Samedi wrapping up people with a snake while spamming a global Zentreya emote. This game to me is what Fortnite is to RussianBadger; a reminder of how weird yet great the internet is. My only nitpick is please buff Baron's everything and nerf Kuku's 3 please and thank you.

Before you download, be aware of what will happen to you when you play your first round of Smite (2/28 times): You get in a lobby with 4 random people that are now your teammates. Why? Because you dont have any friends to play with. The enemy Bakasura kills 2 of you before the first jungle camp even spawns. He proceeds to go 21/2/4 that game.  You end up with a 1/12/3 statline. You teammates flame you. You begin to question yourself if you were even fit to play with and against real people. You are starting to lose yourself. Are you frustrated? Yes, of course. Are you gonna play again? Probably. Will you have to play with 4 other people that grew up with absent parents?  Doubtless.

For real though, Smite is a fun free2play Moba with really f***ing awesome abilites that will leave you in awe when you first see them. Oneshotting people by calling the kraken from the depths with posidon or seeing the enemy gods as small as literal ants while you stomp with Cuthulus ult through their ranks just feels incredible. Also, the 3rd Person Camera seems weird at first, when you come from your typical moba such as League or Dota, but it is really not that bad. So just give it a try, its free. What more do you want?

Very fun to play with friends. but it has a huge learning curve. Also really good players will make smurf accounts so they dont have to play people as good as them, and just stomp on the new player base.

Smite is a MOBA that uses mythological characters, they update often, and experiment with game modes to keep it somewhat fresh. the community can be tough to get along with, but playing with friends is a good way to mitigate some of this.

The most toxic yet fun and challenging game I've played. Playing on your own in solo queuing is a big risk since a lot of players may not be experienced, not care about teamwork, etc. Playing with friends is 10x better.

One of my favorite games, that I have been playing for way too long. The only downside is that they are constantly trying to balance, therefore every time I take a break it seems like I am having to relearn everything again. Maybe that's why I've played it for so long.....
Fun Game but the RNG matchmaking can ruin it tbh some days are fun but whenever a new god comes out i would honestly recommend not playing for like a week until said god can be balanced lol

Rip Smite... Hi-Rez back at it again like Global Agenda. 
just don't play/purchase Hi-Rez products they'll end up like Paladins.
This game is very enjoyable, sometimes a pain in the butt to go against strong players who know what they are doing but overall not a bad game, can't wait to see what other gods come out.

I enjoyed the game for what it was, but I have spent a lot of money on this game on the playstation platform. As many of my other friends have also experienced, Hi-Rez refuses to port over, give a credit, or even assist in any way to get our skins attached to our PC account. 
I dont even care about our achievements, stats, or anything else really... but I have skins/voicepacks you cannot unlock/achieve anymore on the playstation account, and to flat out refuse to assist in any way is poor customer service imo, and honestly ruined this game for me and my friends. 
There are large reddit threads with the same comments.

The game can cause negative emotions because it has buggy achievements and many inadequate random players who leave the game prematurely or press the surrender button, thereby destroying it. And then there are unique creatures who do nothing and just write in the chat while standing AFK, how strategists they are, and actually play worse than the bots in the game.

Congrats Hi-Rez. You messed up! AGAIN! You don't seem to ever learn a thing from your mistakes! And you made so many mistakes over the years! Here's a list of your PC failures (not even counting the mobile market failures funded using Smite revenue): Global Agenda, Tribes: Ascend, Paladins, Realm Royale, Rogue Company, Divine Knockout and now sadly Smite. You should know by now, based on previous experiences, the game is dead as soon as you stop updating it. You could just transfer everything from Smite 1 to Smite 2 (like Overwatch did) and have your playerbase "just deal with it", but no, that would've been too easy. And would come with no additional revenue. In stead you decided to keep both, so you can milk your playerbase again for the same content. And then just stopped updates for Smite 1. 

I call you out on your BS, Hi-Rez! Shame on you! I hope you enjoy the dumpster fire, watch it all burn!

SMITE has been one of the few games that I have spent so many hours of my life playing. When I started out I really enjoyed the action and skill based combat that it presented, it isn't like most MOBAs where your abilities are all about the strategy and timing, no in this one you actually have to aim most of them as well. It just adds that extra level of fun and excitement that I love in games.

Sadly that was then and this is now, with every single update the game becomes more broken, the lag increases, weird crap happens because your client and the server can't agree on whether or not you've used your abilities and every new god released is just a much stronger pick than your old favorites that you love to play. I'm really sad to have seen this game go downhill but it's just broken to me now, it was so much fun but I cannot recommend where it is at now.

Being someone who has played smite for many years, i feel i can give a good, full review on this game. 

SUMMARY- Between the community being one of the worst a person could ever be a part of, and the administration at hirez studios being ran by what seems to be childish adult idiots which in my opinion seems to be the equivalent of the first person who thought ''hey im going to go tug and suck on this cows nipple and see where that takes me'' the horrible ban policy, the horribly implemented cheat system, and the fact they just ban for even false reports or even running things like mod organizer for skyrim or overclocking your hardware (cpu for example) and in some cases people only playing for one day and just being banned with no explanation, others playing for months or over a year and randomly being banned (losing 100s of dollars invested and a lot of time) with no reasons given, this will get a thumbs down from me. for a more in depth review, see below-

GRAPHICS-graphics are decent at best, and the integration is just horrible. it pushes your cpu and gpu much harder than it should for what it is. meaning this is a very poorly made game on the inside, wrapped up with decent graphics. 

COMMUNITY- On to the community. it is by FAR one of the worst communities to exist online (and yes i am in cs:go, call of duty, and battlefield) this community IS FULL of the most ignorant, violent, angry people i have ever seen. from people placing death threats, harassing you, Doxing you, and acting like children over bad matches. EXPECT- racism, sexism, trolling, people getting upset and leaving ALL the time, harassment, and yes, sadly even hacking. does hirez do anything about it most of the time? NOPE! they allow all of the above despite the suspension and ban policy, and its usually the innocent ones who get banned because trolls harassing them, and reporting them on multiple accounts or some other stupid reason. 

SUPPORT- Support is horrible, hirez is ran by adult children who seem to pick and choose who they like, then ban or suspend others. in many cases for no reason and they rarely respond to tickets. the system they use called hipatchservice.exe runs on your pc constantly, It is suppose to check if you are cheating or running cheating software, it has been known to ban people for using skyrim mod manager, or even simply overclocking your CPU as they consider it a form of speed hacking. they can, and will ban over even the smallest thing and despite what they say, do NOT investigate reports. they simply go on what is reported, so if you play a game and have a bad game, your team reports you. you will most likely be banned for ''feeding'' or something stupid. in turn losing any money you have spent in this game.  They care nothing about the community and make the most stupid choices on patch updates, They spent a whole year perfecting smite to the point of patch updates needing to fix minor things, everything was perfect and balanced, and in one single patch, someone in hirez thought ''hey lets mess it up for us and everyone'' and changed many items stats and abilities. changed cost, ruining builds for many gods, causing HUGE imbalances again, nerfing gods they spent a year getting balanced while buffing others. in turn ruining the game for many. 

IN CLOSING- i still play every now and then, but only because i have invested so much time and money into it over the years. whenever i give it another chance, within the first 3 games, at least 1 person ruins the gaming mood then im done again. Havent played much but am loving so far. 

keeps disapiring out my libary and have to go through the store to play it. this seems more of a steam problem rather then anything to do with the actual game. 

SO much better quaulity then 99% of free games.
fun game, great list of gods to play with and mix of strategies to try out. good team based CO OP game MOBA. only issue is poor match making and toxic community

Game is fun, but Hi-Rez can eat a ♥♥♥♥.

They just decided to go the AI route for VA and that's pure cancer.

DO NOT BUY THIS GAME!!!  Hi-rez is an incredibly sketchy company.  A buddy of mine got banned and because hes also my roommate my account got banned.  They take weeks to get back to you whenever you have an issue and their website is designed to make creating a support ticket a headache.  They also act as though they care about not being toxic...  They do not.  They profit off of it by creating you rock, cancel that merchandise.

While I no longer wish to use the harsh language I did before, the sentiment remains.
Once upon a time, this game was truly so much fun, I started when it came out on PS4 - especially in a group of friends. The variety was good, had solid lore to build off of, abilities felt nice even when overpowered. I both loved & hated old Loki.
But the greed was too much to watch - the events started out nice, nothing amazing nothing terrible but rewards for being active - playing the game. Then they shifted to "buying in" to the event, which led to being allowed to spend a lot more money, which felt intolerable but I played for a bit in spite because I loved the chaos of Assault mode. But over & over again seeing nothing but money funnels in a game you spend a lot of time in, that you really enjoy, well it just feels like a slap in the face.
It's been a long time since I've played, with the release of Smite 2 I gave up completely & accepted greed won, love for the players lost.
With no 1 to 1 transfer of purchased content, yes I saw what you offered it was not enough, it just reeks of more greed.
"I know this game has been out for years & you've spent a lot of money, but we'll only give you a fraction back because we made a "new" version of the game and don't feel you need everything you've bought." k.
If I was given everything I had in a new version of the same game, I would've given Smite 2 a chance - but you didn't.

Hopefully, for the sake of anyone left playing these games, something changed in my time gone and you're able to enjoy the game the way I did before but I doubt it.

You hate yourself? You feel useless? No? You will get laught at by the enemy and you will get insulted by your mates because of your sheer existence. This game will fill you up with so much hate that you will install a macro-programm with the only purpouse to laught on other people. Now when an enemy ever tries to laught at you...oh boy, i hope god will be with him...the next time you will kill him you will never release that button of your macro-programm. Even tough it means your own death. That little joy; Seeing your enemy suffer as much as you do makes this all worth it.

Smite is a great MOBA that I have enjoyed since beta and have logged well over 1200 hours and spent hundreds of dollars buying ingame skins over the past few years. As of the latest patch they disregarded the community outrage over adding EAC, which is known to be as invasive as spyware, to smite. I would not recommend anyone install this game as it will infect your computer with EAC. If Hirez removes EAC from their game I will update this review to reflect my opinion of this game.

Honestly really amazing game and probably the least toxic moba i've played by far. There are some balancing issues and balancing patches come in slowly (OP danzabourou for a month)  but other than that and rare network issues I have no complaints

This is by far the funniest, most interesting and most easy to learn MOBA on the market (oh wait it's free). Well it's only free if you intend to unlock gods one by one, which I tried to do until I gave up and paid 20€ for the whole god pack and stuff, but I don't regret this at all.
It's stress-relieving when you're used to play stressful/frustration-based games (Dark Souls III, Dota 2 on [strike] russian [/strike] european servers). I just enjoy this as a badass mythological Street Fighter look alike with tons of dad jokes inside, even though it's full of more or less complex mechanics and the gameplay evolves constantly over the seasons. I just don't play competitive matchmaking for that reason. I WANT TO HAVE FUN FUN FUN FUN FUUUUN. Also this MOBA is rather fast-paced compared to LoL or Dota (which I played both). XP goes fast, items are more diverse, but it's the only thing that seem to matter to players. ITEM BUIlDS ARE KEY. Like if you run Midguardian Mail on Chaac, you're a useless ♥♥♥♥ coz you need Ice Hammer instead, or whatever. But otherwise, community is much better than the one of the 2 older brothers I mentionned. (Getting invaded by russians from time to time, but it's still viable to play on Euro servers.)

Best free game available in my opinion. thousands of hours of playability. never have to spend a dime if you dont want to. i did purchase the god pack. but i played for a year or more before i purchased god pack. is fun with or without god pack.

tricky and some times toxic but over all fun with firends but can get annoy and its kinda the same thing over and over if you stick to a small group of gods that you use but fun for sure i keep finding myself going back to it after breaks 
edit(2022) i still stand by this
A much better league of legends, the pagan in me enjoys all the different gods and goddesses!
For like a decade old this game is pretty good. It's no Spyro: Year of the Dragon but its pretty good.

The Game has its ups and downs. After playing the game from beta to now, I can Say the games improved in quality. The game has evolved to another level compared to previous seasons and i love the collaborations they have been pumping out recently. Great Game overall. Oh and 2k hours in and 1k $ burned pretty fun with friends.

I started playing SMITE just as season 2 was ending. When season 3 came, the maps were changed, there were new gods, and the community, well, it stayed the same. SMITE is one of those games that has a gamemode for everyone, arena if you enjoy team fights, conquest if you prefer a long, tactical match, duels if you want to bet your skill against the world, and many more gamemodes that I'm not going to go over. It's not quite a moba, but in my heart, it will always have a place as a moba, and I thank the devs for making such a great game for me to waste nearly 800 hours of my life playing. just 1 word of advice, bring Xbalanque into jungle, we Xba mains must shift the meta!

This game delves into a whole new level of BS. Ive had it since mid beta (right when Nemesis was added) and I like a lot of the changes that they've added in over the years, but sometimes Hi-Rez just doesnt know when a god is absolutely broken. But thankfullly its actually a free to play and spending money willl just get you skins or just unlock all the gods at once. 
7/10 worth playing but prepare for a lovel of broken BS that youve never seen before

Amazing game a true batttle ground of the gods. Being free to play the game allows you to get a true tast of what it realy is about. And with new gods in rotaion you can try out new veriety of play styles before you ever purchase the full package. I highly recomend atleast trying it out for yourself.
Wow this game is fun Lel i use to play lol still do lol but now i kinda play this lol isnt it funny lol this game is pretty good but i could be better if u know what i mean lel
It is honestly better that league or dota, Also It reminds me of world of warcraft pvp but better.

Erstens.

Ich kann das Spiel empfehlen, habe nun ca, 300 Stunden, also insgesamt, 
dochder Entwickler HiRez verkackt das Spielt immer mehr.

Smite, Lol und ich.

Das Spiel SMITE  ist, wenn man es sich grob vorstellt,
ein LoL in Ego-Perspektive. Dank meinem Kumpel weiß ich auch,
dass ein paar Charakter mit Lol Champs ähnlichkeit haben und 
somit relativ gleich sind. Ich selber mag mein Leauge of Legends, 
dies liegt wohl 
1. an meiner mangelten Erfahrung 
2. An meinem (fast) 
nie staatgefundenen Erfolgsmomentan .
3. Man bekommt auf die Fresse, ich noob zumindest.

SMITE an sich.

So, hier kommt der eigendliche Grund wieso ich SMITE nicht umbedingt
empfehlen kann: Die Faulheit der Entwickler und die Balance des Spiels.
Fangen wir mit Göttern (Charakteren) wie Anubis an seine Ultimative
Fähichkeit ist ein Strahl aus seinen Augen zu schießen, dieser macht auf max. level 
ca. 6000 Damage. Zum vergleich: Nicht-Beschützer haben im Durchschnitt 2000 Leben.
So kann man mit diesem Charakter bspw. sehr einfach die Bosse auf der Karte töten
um sich diese Boni zu erhachen. 

Als nächstes kommt mein lieblingsspruch.
Was ist ein Krieger (Klasse)?: ADC (Damage) + Beschützer (Typ-Der-Viel-Aushält)
Was ist ein Beschützer (Klasse)?:  ADC (Damage) + Beschützer+ (Typ-Der-Sehr-Viel-Aushält)
Dies, und der Fakt, dass jeder Charakter überall, irgendwie rauskommt, stören mich.
Ein Beschützer (bspw. Bacchus) mit 45 Magischer Kraft (Damage) SOLL KEINE 500 DAMAGE
MIT SEINER ULTI MACHEN! 
Charakter die, und davon gibt es nicht viele, Skill brauchen bspw. Silvanus (Tank),
werden generft / verschlechtert. Er ist einer der Tank's die nicht OP sind
und Schaden verteilen wie ein aufgekoster Typ an ner Minigun.

Zitat von seinen letzten Nerfs:
4.1: "Decreased Base Healing from 25/30/35/40/45 → 15/20/25/30/35 per tick"
4.2: "Sylvanus: Base damage on Autos reduced from 38 to 35"
Aber OP Charakter werden schön gebufft.

Entwicklung.

Ich verstehe, dass Entwickler Fehler machen, finde ich aber auch völlig inordung,
wenn diese auch bald behoben wurden.
Bei der Patch 4.1 wurde ein neuer Gott eingefügt, namens Morrigan, sie hat eine
Ultimative Fähichkeit sich in jeden belieben Gott zu verwandeln der im Match ist und deren
Items zu übernehmen, diesen Gott kaufte ich mir. Der Bugs sind:
- Nach der Verwandlung kann man max. 2 Sekunden lang keine Fähichkeiten benutzen
- In Scharmützel (3 gegen 3) sind es manchmal 4 Leute in einem Team (bspw. ich übernehe Neith: 
2. Neiths)
Seit ein paar Wochen immernoch nicht behoben.

Was ich aber nicht verstehe ist, wenn Entwickler zu FAUL sind Übersetzungen zu machen,
nicht mal es per Google Translater versuchen. So stehen meistens in der Deutschen Version

The price (the ultimate god pack) is totaly worth it. The only down side in this game is some community members who'll think everyone is a professional. Its rare to have a full team vs team fight in this game , but i would stil highly recommend it.

Great game and not a 'pay to win' type of game. The 'gems' (which is the premium/paid currency) can be obtain via daily logins and you can use them for cosmetics or obtaining other characters (although I strongly do not recommend spending gems on characters since you can obtain the free currency called favour when playing the game). I have personally spent 7 years of my life playing this game and honestly speaking the main things I dislike about this game are: Matchmaking, the servers, the reporting system, and lastly the amount of toxicity in casual games. 

Matchmaking:
It is utter garbage since you can either get lower skilled players or highly skilled ones on your team whilst the enemy team is just as bad as your teammates or them being a lot better then your entire team making the game feel like garbage. 

The Servers:
They can be consistent at times but on other days they are ghetto since it would be either you lagging or your opponents lagging. 

The reporting system: 
As of the moment they are now "taking action" but there are so many that abuse this (especially in ranked).

Toxicity in casuals:
Too many people are toxic for no reason and honestly it's casuals so if you don't want to get 'tilted' over some casual games just do ranked. Since casuals isn't fun due to the matchmaking being pretty bad.

it's holding up a lot better than most of the other games that came out around the same time. Surprisingly it's even holding itself better than Paladins at the moment, (let's not mention Realm Royale).

I actually have a lot more hours on the game than it shows on here. I already had 750 hours before I switched to the steam version. Been playing since season 1 back in 2014. The game has gone through many many changes over the years, most of them being for the better. The game has a very good free to play model. You can unlock every god in the game with in game currency, and all the mastery skins and teir 1 skins with in game currency as well. On  top of that if you get your daily long in bonus every day, you earn paid for currency at the end of the week which you can save up to buy voice packs or higher teir skins that you want. They also have event weekends fairly often that let you earn even more paid for currency. Overall this game is and has been one of my favorite games of all time, and I don't see myself quitting any time soon. I highly reccommend the game to everyone out there.

Easy Anti Cheat, an invasive and useless anti-cheating measure, is being implemented in the next patch. This does nothing to fix the only (rare) form of cheating in the game, speedhacking, and will only hurt legitimite players.

This is the only MOBA I play, simply because the over the shoulder viewpoint makes the game much more exciting and action packed than the traditional top-down view of LOL or DOTA. There are regular balance patches as well as patches that add aesthetic content as well as new characters. Definitely a fun game.
My one bit of advice for any new players coming into the game would be, don't be afraid of conquest as a game-mode. For some reason it's locked until your account gets to level 5, which puts a few players off. 
Content and gameplay is excellent and I would definitely reccommend it to anyone.

I do not see Smite as a worthy moba game, so I came here for the casual Arena experience. What I had in my 1200+ hours is nothing but pain and humiliation. I cannot take it anymore, it leaves me in a suicidal mood every time. Match-making is non-existent here.

I wholeheartedly hope this game dies.

At this point in time i have 799 hours logged. I'm gonna do a short and sweet review of the game and try and list a few things that are diffrent than other MOBA'S and hopefully give you guys some insight for people looking to get into the game. 

Smite is a 3rd person MOBA unlike most MOBA's, which are usually top down, now with that being said makes the game imo a whole diffrent type of MOBA and the way i prefer to play a MOBA.

I've only played around 3 matches of LoL, and as ive said before i prefer the 3rd person aspect, top down games just arent my style, so i cant really compare this to other MOBA's

Now. to get to the basics, you have your Guardians,Mages,Warriors,Hunters and Assassins, All of which are all fun to play my preffered class would usually be a guardian or mage seeing that not many people choose to play guardians and that hunters are far to generic.

Hunters- Hunters are your standard point and hold left click for the most part they are effective early game but most useful in late game scenarios to shut down the lower health gods easily and also beat down your enamy Tank.

Mages- All around good class used to shut down most all god types most having a very diffrent kit and reasonably diffrent from each other enough to realize you're playing another god and not a re-skin of another.

Guardians- Guardians are your preferred tank class although warriors can also be very tanky most guardians are fun in their own way and all have a play in the game but less than the other classes imo unless the right hands are behind them.

Warriors- Warriors are very fun to play alot of them have good kits and some of them being very overpowered in the right hands and some being huge game changers for your 5v5 fights in late game joust.

Assassins- Assassins are very crucial to your Conquest matches (5v5) and quite a few of them are left untouched during seasons due to a few being Overpowered, They're used for your jungling and to gank lane as most moba's.

Now that thats over with i'd like to touch base on a few of the things i like and dont like about smite.

Pro's- Visually the game looks great every one of the gods are very detailed, maps look great and the spells and abilities really bring all of it to life during matches.

3rd person aspect- as ive said before i dont enjoy top down games and if you do thats fine you may still like smite regardless, Top down just isnt my cup of tea.

Community- All in all the community is great in a whole if you join in solo for the most part if there are people playing solo as well they are usually nice, although you can have teams of clan people who are toxic just as there would be toxic people in other Moba's but smite seems to have less toxicity than other Moba's too me.

Updates-Smite is regularly updated with new content Very Often, in the last year It's seen around 11 new gods meaning we see new gods almost every month, Gods usually stay relatively balanced with your select few that are more overpowered than others, but High-Rez stays on top of it.

Gods-All of the gods feel diffrent to me very few of them have simialar skills and for the most part all of the gods in the game are fun and ANY of them can be good if they are in the right hands.

Cons-Unfortunately there are some bad things about smite and i will tell you what my biggest problems with the game are.

Hunters- Hunters are fun to play and i wont lie i love to play Neith,Ah Muzen Cab, Xblanque and many others, BUT they need to be nerfed unlike League (going by word of mouth) Yes, their abilities help them in fights tremendously but some if not all of them by end game only have to hold down left click to kill anybody, doesnt matter what their opponent is even guardians get melted with full physical protection, I'd suggest removing penetration items from the game, They make being a guardian not fun.

Guardians- There are alot of fun guardians in the game BUT, as ive said in the previous statement Hunters can melt them, just as any other class can in the right hands, they just dont feel tanky enough most of the time no matter how much armor you build, and even at this point you can build for damage so you do no damage and die regardless due to the lack of damage resistance from said armor. Although there are many guardians that do alot of damage and are seemingly tankier than others leaving the other guardians hanging out to dry.

Now that ive gotten my two biggest gripes over classes out of the way ill list a few diffrent other urks i have with the game.

I've previously stated that there isnt alot of toxicity in the game but, I like to play assault alot and i've done this before just in case someone reading this recognizes my name, Being toxic or talking crap in this game-mode makes you look like a huge dbag, You're not playing Ranked-Conquest. Stop acting like it, Thats my only problem with the community is that i can understand when someone is purposely throwing the match/feeding but, if you watch and see that they're actually trying, give them a chance if you lose at the end of the match say gg, nice try they may be new and your actions could be the reason that they stop playing the game, meaning we're losing people from an amazing game that already has a much smaller player base than the likes of Dota 2, or LoL.

Last thing is the server downtime, I want this game to grow, and i want the playerbase to grow also, But, it seems like and moreso here recently that the servers have been down quite a bit. in The last month there was upwards of a week (altogether) that i couldnt play because of server downtime. I've read many articles stating that it's Because of DDOS from other developers, I would hope that another company wouldnt stoop that low. My problem with all of this is not that im missing out on playing but afraid that at some point people will get tired of it and go to something else, and im sure im not the only person that feels this way. So High-Rez you've made one of my favorite games of all time, Please, try and fix this problem.

Thats the end of all of my pro's/con's and gripes of the game Hopefully this will help you to decide to play or not to play and it answered some questions for you.

I'll update my review if anything else sticks out to me. 

Favorite Gods-Vulcan,Agni,Neith,Khepri,Poseidon.

I played this game since closed beta around 6 or 7 years ago. I mastered almost every god until about a year or two ago and played hundreds of hours of every game mode with friends and alone.
They game used to be good, it had great direction and things that made it quite unique and it still does but recently every patch or major update is for more skins or a giant event that requires you to spend money to get a cool looking frame or skin. New gods are released with horrible balance, frequently overpowered so that people are inclined to spend money to dominate games with that god early in the release. Following by patches that nerf the god so that they either become balanced or underpowered. With so many new gods coming out nowadays, it is clear that the smite team can't balance them all. 

tl;dr is unique but isn't balanced and sometimes isn't fun because of that. clear priority on making money which is fair but results in a spend all the money meta game

I recently started playing smite through steam, I have 1022 hrs on record. I started playing because of a friend, I wasn't interested in this game at all back in the day, Smite is a great game if you want to play with a party of 2 or 3 because of the competitive aspects of the game, if you're more of a casual player you can play with up to 5 people. If you're someone that doesn't like to deal with a bunch of stuff in the game (game mechanics, items, buffs, debuffs, passives, etc) then this isn't a game for you, if you're into strategic-well coordinated games, then I think you'll enjoy this. Sadly if you're a solo player you can have a really hard time in ranked, eventually you'll meet people in-game that can help you rank up. I would recommend this game, tbh most of the times I forget it's a FTP game, it's really well done and the devs love to keep the game updated with game balance and skins.

SMITE is an incredibly fun and addicting 3rd person MOBA that is perfect for those who are looking for a change from your typical MOBA. 

Smite's unique camera angle alone is enough to set it apart from other MOBAs, and developer Hi-Rez didn't stop there. Each map is polished, every God looks and sounds great, and exceptional Match of the Day modes offer plenty of variety. 

It's not as bad but still crashes and disconnects frequently yes, the game has possibly the worst hardware I've ever seen. Players get disconnected every 2 or 3 matches on average,

The lore on each God is surpringly extensive and accurate. The fact the feature even exists makes me happy as does the entire premise of the game: Many Gods from many pantheons facing off against eachother in battle.

really cool game but it is not noob friendly, after a few lv ups you get matched up with experienced players. and how the hell do i shut up that ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ anoying tutorial chick, i dont see anywhere to turn it off in settings.

Honestly, I really wanted to enjoy this game, and at its core I do.  It does enough to set itself apart from the other MOBAs, and what I have seen of the roster seemed fairly interesting.  My issue with Smite sadly isn't the game itself at all, its the playerbase.  After playing several games vs AI and getting familiar with the feel of the game, I jump into a pvp match, where I am then berated for 20 minutes because my character was in the 'wrong lane'.  I explained that this was my first game, and I was unaware of any laning metas, but was then told that doesn't matter, and is no excuse.  To my knowledge, Smite does not tell you which lane to play which character.  Initially, I wrote this off as a disgruntled player, but quickly I realized that all the players I was being matched up with were far more seasoned than myself.  Instead of matching me with other new players or players of similar skill level, it was putting me with players who far outreached my skill level.  This left me in an awkward position, where I could either slowly attempt to learn, and drag down everyone's game who got matched with me, or I could just stop playing, and I feel it would be selfish to keep playing.

Smite has been one of those games that i have been using to fill my addiction gap. Spent waaaay too many hours playing it and i think i have a pretty good understanding of the game and how i feel about it so knowing this i shall now go and list everything in the pros and cons of the game. Tl;dr at the bottom (he he he bottom)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PROS:
-The game itself: Mobas have a very strict formula. Top down, 3 lanes and 2 jungles. BUT WHAT IF WE SHIFTED THE CAMERA BEHIND THE ACTUAL PLAYABLE CHARACTERS? It might sound stupid but it makes the game soo much more fun. Theres something here for everybody.

-Always a mode ready to go: Smite has 6 maps + a daily changed "Mode of the day" The maps and their game modes are: 
Joust - 3v3 map with a single lane some jungle camps and a miniboss thats actualy easier too beat then the camps.
Arena - 5v5 map with no lane just a big ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ circle that you punch eachother in the face in until your tickets get sold out.
Conquest - The most basic 5v5 match there is.(cant thing of anything to add here)
Clash - Like joust but with 2 lanes and 5v5.
Assault - 1 lane, 5v5, no base healing, no backing, no buying items unless you die. Win only by killing the enemy team until they come back with every item in the game and destroy your ass.
Siege - SIEGE MAINS WHERE YOU AT?! like clash but 4v4. Most underplayed game mode out there.
MOTD-s - Not gonna write every single one ♥♥♥♥ even i dont remember all of them and i have no life!

-The best community out there: A major factor in any online game is the people who play it. Usualy its never good but smite has some of the best, most helpful people i have met in an online game.

-The VGS system: Voice communication in videogames has always only been 2 things: Horrible and "your mom" jokes. Now imagine instead of voice communication you have a set of commands that YELL AT YOU AND MAKE YOUR MOM JOKES.

-The fact that you are literaly playing gods: Makes you live your fantasy of being the prettiest girl out there.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CONS: 
-The stability and servers: Oh boy my favorite part here goes. The game runs like ♥♥♥♥ if you dont meet the recommended requirements and/or have it installed on an ssd. Some freezes here and there, crashes and the servers my god the servers. They havent changed in 4 years. Do they hinder the game itself? Yes and no, sometimes just a random freeze can really ruin your play and make you regret living in the first place.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Its one of the best games i have played and makes me glad that I found it in the first place. Fully recommend it too anybody I meet.

I mean, the game at its core feels really magical but for the most part its sorta generic. 

You got the classic MOBA set up, interesting mechanics and Gods. But the overall design feels like...feels cartoony and childish. Even the community at times, is really cringey. Like REALLY annoying, foolish and sometimes aggresive. But thats not really a SMITE problem, its more of a MOBA problem. 

Beta smite felt more authentic, more serious of a tone, more graphic too. Now its sorta restricted (Due to partnerships and whatnot) but overall its still doing okay. The Gods are fun, overall gamemodes are entertaining and its replayable. 

If you wanna play a MOBA without the clickies, without the million Gods or Items, without insanely broken characters (SMITE has its moments though), then this game is probably for you.

If you wanna play a MOBA that is stress free, community friendly and overall a uniquely fresh take on a old format (MOBA) then this might not be for you.


Overall, game is fun but man does it really blow chunks sometimes. It has the CSGO syndrome (Gambling and most gods have rare, limited edition skins.).
